Median MT-Bench score of 8.0 (0-10 scale, single-answer grading across 8 domains: Writing, Roleplay, Reasoning, Math, Coding, Data Extraction, STEM, Humanities), judged by Claude 3.7 Sonnet. From AWS's official ML Blog post 'Benchmarking Amazon Nova: A comprehensive analysis through MT-Bench and Arena-Hard-Auto' (published July 24, 2025), directly fetched and confirmed: 'Both Amazon Nova Lite and Nova Micro achieved respectable median scores of 8.0.' This is a genuinely new benchmark (mt-bench) not previously tracked for this model. Note: the same post also reports an Arena-Hard-Auto pairwise Bradley-Terry score for Nova Lite of 6.51-6.98 (25th-75th percentile, CI -0.22/+0.25), but that is reported as a range rather than a single point score, so it was not added as a discrete result.

AWS Generative AI Innovation Center blog post (published 2025-07-24), single-answer grading with Anthropic's Claude 3.7 Sonnet as judge (0-10 scale). Nova Micro tied Nova Lite for the lowest median score among the four Nova models. Not previously tracked for this model. Same scale-mismatch caveat on Arena-Hard-Auto as noted for Nova Premier applies (custom 0-10 Bradley-Terry, not this corpus's 0-100 'arena-hard' scale) -- deliberately excluded.

AWS Generative AI Innovation Center blog post (published 2025-07-24), single-answer grading with Anthropic's Claude 3.7 Sonnet as judge (0-10 scale). Nova Premier had the highest median score of the four Nova models (8.0-8.6 range), with a 1.5-point min-max spread (7.94-9.47). Not previously tracked for this model. Note: the same post's Arena-Hard-Auto figures use a custom 0-10 normalized Bradley-Terry scale (reference model pinned at 5.0), not the standard 0-100 win-rate scale used elsewhere in this corpus's 'arena-hard' slug, so those were deliberately NOT added here to avoid a scale mismatch.

AWS Generative AI Innovation Center blog post (published 2025-07-24), single-answer grading with Anthropic's Claude 3.7 Sonnet as judge (0-10 scale). Nova Pro scored second-highest of the four Nova models with a wider 2.7-point min-max spread (6.44-9.13). Not previously tracked for this model. Same scale-mismatch caveat on Arena-Hard-Auto as noted for Nova Premier applies here too (custom 0-10 Bradley-Terry, not comparable to this corpus's 0-100 'arena-hard' scale) -- deliberately excluded.

MT-Bench (raw/original scoring), Inflection-2.5 column of a comparison table on Inflection AI's official Inflection-2.5 launch page. Rendered as a vector-outlined SVG image (framerusercontent.com/images/37manVrAmMD8r6wIe4kFckmtq0.svg); confirmed by direct browser rendering (full table -- MT-Bench / MT-Bench Corrected: Inflection-2.5=8.41/8.64, GPT-3.5=7.99/7.9, GPT-4=9.02/8.79). Inflection AI's page text explains they found ~25% of MT-Bench's reasoning/math/coding examples had flawed reference solutions and released a corrected variant (8.64 for Inflection-2.5); the raw/original 8.41 figure is used here as the standard-methodology score comparable to other MT-Bench entries in this corpus (e.g. GPT-3.5 Turbo's existing mt-bench=7.94 from the original Zheng et al. paper), with the self-reported 'corrected' 8.64 variant noted here rather than tracked as a separate entry since no distinct taxonomy slug exists for it.

Japanese MT-Bench (localized/translated MT-Bench, GPT-4-judged, 8 categories x 10 questions, averaged), Llama-3-ELYZA-JP-8B score from ELYZA's official June 26, 2024 Japanese launch blog post (same URL already used in this record's specSources for releaseDate/openness). The number appears only in an embedded comparison-chart image on that page (not machine-text-extractable via automated fetch), so it was cross-verified via two independent third-party transcriptions of the identical chart that both agree: chowagiken.co.jp (https://www.chowagiken.co.jp/future-studio/llama3elyza_jp8b/, explicitly stated as excerpted from ELYZA's note) and ascii.jp (https://ascii.jp/elem/000/004/208/4208543/2/) -- both list Llama-3-ELYZA-JP-8B at 3.655 (ELYZA Tasks 100) / 7.78 (Japanese MT-Bench), versus GPT-3.5 Turbo 3.475/8.54, Gemini 1.0 Pro 3.515/8.49, Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct base 3.000/7.49, and Llama-3-ELYZA-JP-70B 4.070/9.08 in the same chart. Mapped to the 'mt-bench' taxonomy slug (Japanese-localized variant, clearly labeled here) following existing corpus precedent at corpus/models/preferred-networks/plamo-100b.json, which records Japanese MT-Bench under the same slug since no separate Japanese-MT-Bench slug exists in the taxonomy. The companion ELYZA Tasks 100 score (3.655) is NOT recorded because no matching taxonomy slug exists for it (same precedent: plamo-100b.json deliberately omits its analogous 'JHumanEval' score for lack of a slug).
